q: Out of the following, which type of interaction is responsible for the stabilisation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo></math>helix structure of proteins?
question: Out of the following, which type of interaction is responsible for the stabilisation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo></math>helix structure of proteins?
solution:
Hydrogen bonding is responsible for the stacking of α-helix structure of protein.

The α-helix structure of proteins is stabilized by intramolecular H-bonding between C−O of one amino acid residue and the N−H of the fourth amino acid residue in the chain.

V= Number of valence electron present in central atom

question: Consider the elements <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Mg</mi><mo>,</mo></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Al</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Si</mi><mo>,</mo></math> the correct increasing order of their first ionisation enthalpy is:
solution:
In general from left to right in a period, ionisation enthalpy increases due to effective nuclear charge increases. but due to extra stability of half filled and full filled electronic configuration, required ionisation enthalpy is more from neighbouring elements.
i.e. first ionisation enthalpy order is

Al<Mg<Si<S<P

q: The gas released during anaerobic degradation of vegetation may lead to:
question: The gas released during anaerobic degradation of vegetation may lead to:
solution:
The gas CH4 evolved due to anaerobic degradation of vegetation, which causes global warming and cancer.

Anaerobic degradation means the breakdown in the absence of oxygen. During anaerobic degradation of vegetation, CH4 is produced, which is responsible for global warming as it absorbs sun’s heat. It is also known to cause cancer.

solution:
Aniline on protonation gives anilinium ion which is meta directing. So considerable amount of meta product is formed.

Direct nitration of aniline yields tarry oxidation products in addition to the nitro derivatives. solution:
B and C will contain three digit numbers of the form 9k+2 and 9k+l respectively. We need to find sum of all elements in the set B∪C effectively.

Now, SB∪C=SB+SC-SB∩C where S(k) denotes sum of elements of set k.

Also, B=101,110,……,992

∴ SB=1002101+992=54650

Case-I: If l=2

then B∩C=B

∴ SB∪C=SB

which is not possible as given sum is

274×400=109600.

Case-II If l≠2

then B∩C=ϕ

∴ SB∪C=SB+SC=400×274

⇒ 54650+∑k=11109k+l=109600

⇒ 9∑k=11110k+∑k=11110l=54950

⇒ 9100211+110+l100=54950

⇒ 54450+100l=54950

⇒ l=5

solution:
Let x-31=y-42=z-52=t

⇒ x=3+t, y=2t+4, z=2t+5

For a point of intersection with x+y+z=17, then it satisfies the equation of a plane. 

⇒3+t+2 t+4+2 t+5=17

⇒ 5t=5⇒t=1

⇒ The point of intersection is 4,6,7.

Then, distance between 1,1,9 and 4,6,7 is 9+25+4=38.

So, the direction ratios of normal to the required plane are 11,1,17 and plane passes through 1, 2,-3. So, the equation of plane is

question:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I: Two photons having equal linear momenta have equal wavelengths.

Statement II: If the wavelength of the photon is decreased, then the momentum and energy of a photon will also decrease.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below.
options: Both Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are false
Both Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are true
Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is true but Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is false
Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is false but Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is true
solution:
If linear momentum are equal then wavelength also equal.

The momentum p and energy E of a photon is given by,

p=hλ, E=hcλ; where c is speed of light and h is Plank's constant.

On decreasing wavelength, momentum and energy of photon increases.

solution:
F.B.D. of the block is shown in the diagram

fr-mg=0             ...1
